_README: Hand-authored show-note EXTRAS and overrides, keyed by episode GUID. Merged on top of the auto-imported feed notes (episodeNotesFeed.json), and manual fields win. Per entry: { summary: string|string[] (a written overview), references: [{label, url, note?}] (texts & thinkers + links), pages?: string, drafted?: bool, overview?/chapters?: override the feed }. Keys starting with '_' are ignored. The three entries below are AUTO-DRAFTED samples (Nietzsche S5E9, Derrida S4E1, Hegel S1E1) for format review.

e731648d-62cb-4d81-90a7-0a2be49333a4.summary: Reading Book IV of The Gay Science, the conversation turns to the tone of affirmative thinking — amor fati, Nietzsche's "brief habits," and the cheerful science as a practice of living rather than a doctrine to be argued for. Thinking becomes therapeutic: less refuting an opponent than situating them within a larger narrative, and cultivating a capacity to sense.

references.label: Gilles Deleuze
references.note: on the "refrain," repetition, speeds and slowness

7c4404c3-1fc6-4730-bdec-5c9e4d9ed16c.summary: The Derrida season opens. After a short intro to the season (and the new intro music), we take up "Différance" — the components of Derrida's central (non)concept, how he diverges from Heidegger, and what the essay says about the dynamics of contemporary theory.

cd7d14d9-7f14-4dbe-b61c-dea295f73b38.summary: The maiden episode. We start with styles of reading and teaching, academic dispositions and authority, then move into the Phenomenology from §13 of the Preface — Hegel's Understanding versus Self-Consciousness, and the conditions of possibility for scientific knowledge.
